error_patterns: Three systematic patterns dominate, all confirmed present in this batch: (1) M04_MISE_EN_DEMEURE over-application: Hunter tagged M04 on 7 consecutive fiches of the sas-capy-la-teste-de-buch-avenue-andre-ampere / 2022-10-11 inspection where the corpus contains zero occurrences of "demeure" anywhere in the fiche (verified by full-text search across all 24 M04-tagged records in the batch) -- these are plain injunctions with numeric delays, not MEDs. A related variant appears in 2 fiches of the 2024-02-07 capy inspection where M04 was applied to a QUOTED conditional threat from the prior year's report rather than an active MED for the current point -- corrected to m_MENACE instead. (2) m_MENACE under-application: the sas-distillerie-douence 2023-02-28 inspection closes every open-item fiche with the identical sentence 'L'absence de réalisation des actions supra expose l'exploitant à des suites administratives de type mise en demeure' -- a textbook conditional MED threat -- yet Hunter never tagged m_MENACE across any of the 5 fiches carrying it (f01, f03, f05, f06, f07). (3) m_DELAI over-application: 6 records across 4 different companies had m_DELAI attached to demands with no numeric deadline anywhere in the text (contextual phrases like 'cette année' were mistaken for imposed delays). Beyond these three flagged patterns, additional False-M14 errors were found: Hunter repeatedly classified administrative/regulatory gaps (AP missing a provision), pure documentation gaps (plan coherence, absent traçabilité system), and absence-of-measurement issues (no monitoring in place, no noise measurement taken) as M14_CONSTAT_TECHNIQUE (physically observed NC) when no physical degradation was actually described in the text -- corrected to M09_PAC, M01_DOCUMENTATION, or M02_MESURE depending on the specific gap. One clear gravity miscalibration (G3 should be G4 per the 'MED active + NC technique concomitante' rule) and 3 stage miscalibrations (S01_CONSTAT should be S02_INJONCTION given an explicit numeric-delay demand in the constats body) were also found and corrected, all within the same MED-related record cluster. No instances of R10_TENSION misuse were found (Hunter never used R10 in this batch, so pattern (a) from the audit brief does not apply here).
verdict: Batch quality is below the project's <15% error target: 25/60 audited fiches (42%) required at least one field correction, concentrated almost entirely in one recurring company (sas-capy-la-teste-de-buch, 4 inspection dates) whose report format apparently confused Hunter -- quoted historical MED/threat language from prior-year sections was repeatedly read as this year's active enforcement action, and 'Demande à formuler' fields left blank in the primary field caused Hunter to miss substantive demands embedded further down in the document. Outside this cluster (sarl-les-pierres, sarl-moulin-du-caouley, sas-brochard, sas-des-vignobles, sas-j-dubourg, sas-la-compagnie-des-pruneaux, sas-carrefour), Hunter's tagging was accurate and the sampled high-confidence records held up under spot-check. All corrections apply cleanly against the STRICT code-validity rules -- post-correction validation against the full taxonomy vocabulary found 0 invalid codes, 0 cross-axis contamination (mechanisms/modifiers), 0 multi-valued single fields, and 0 missing fields across all 130 records.
